seoTitle: How to Make an Organizational Chart in Canva
metaDescription: Build a clear organizational chart in Canva using customizable templates, ideal for onboarding, team structure, and project planning.
intro: An organizational chart helps clarify roles and reporting lines, whether you're onboarding new hires or restructuring a team. This tutorial covers using Canva's templates and drag-and-drop editing to build a chart that fits your organization.

steps.heading: Understand organizational charts
steps.body: Get a quick overview of what an org chart communicates and why it matters for a business or team.

steps.heading: Get started in Canva
steps.body: Open Canva and locate the tools relevant to building an organizational chart.

steps.heading: Choose a template
steps.body: Select a starting template that fits your organization's size and structure.

steps.heading: Customize your chart
steps.body: Adjust roles, names, and hierarchy using drag-and-drop editing to reflect your actual team.

steps.heading: Finalize and share
steps.body: Review the finished chart and share it with your team for onboarding or planning purposes.

faq.question: What is an organizational chart used for?
faq.answer: Org charts help clarify roles, improve communication, and support onboarding within a business or team.

faq.question: Can I easily update the chart as my team grows?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva's drag-and-drop editing makes it simple to update diagrams as company structure changes.

faq.question: Is this feature suitable for both startups and large enterprises?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial covers use cases across startups, large enterprises, and academic settings.

faq.question: Does Canva offer collaboration tools for team charts?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva includes collaboration tools that streamline building and sharing org charts across a team.
